_comment: Deterministic, rule-based contamination detection applied to candidate values BEFORE consolidation. Rules act on the citation text ('snippet'), the plant identity and the value. Each is plain string/regex matching in code - no LLM. Every dropped row is logged in outputs/contamination_removed.csv with its rule name.

cross_plant_duplicate._note: If the same leading-N-char snippet is attached to >1 plant, keep only the row whose own city name appears in the snippet; drop the copies. N = parameters.snippet_key_chars.

wrong_entity._note: Snippet names a different company/site than the plant. Each rule: drop if all 'tokens' present in snippet AND (optional) 'require_absent_in_plant_id' is NOT in plant_id.

city_mismatch._note: REPRODUCIBILITY CAVEAT: this list was curated by inspecting THIS dataset, not derived automatically. Drop a row if its snippet contains one of 'tokens' but the plant's city is not 'home_city'. In this dataset only Munich's group figure (7,800 / 6,000) was systematically mis-stamped onto other BMW plants, so the list has a single entry. A fully general city-mismatch rule is possible but re-introduces false positives (e.g. Emden pages that merely mention Bremen) and would need a reviewed decision list.

production_units._note: Drop if the extracted value itself is immediately followed by a units word (regex: <value in de/en number formats>\s*(word)). Catches '25.000 Einheiten des Sprinter' read as a headcount.

job_announcement._note: Hiring plan, not a headcount ('will create N jobs').

historical._note: Founding-era figure ('1,300 workers at the end of the 19th century').

wiki_specific._note: Extra rules applied only to the Wikipedia source, because Wikipedia infoboxes carry group-level and unit figures the archived scrapes do not.
wiki_specific.company_level_note: Porsche Zuffenhausen infobox reports Porsche AG group totals, not the plant.
wiki_specific.wrzesnia_note: Plant did not open until 2016; earlier figures are company/region level.
